name: Fat-Free Gravy
recipeIngredient: Pinch Basil (for beef) or Sage (for turkey)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook bones as for soup, then strain the liquid.
2. Refrigerate overnight or until solid fat on top can be removed with a spoon.
3. Refrigerate meat drippings from the pan and skim off the fat.
4. Combine broth, flour, and cornstarch in a screw-top jar. Shake well or blend to create a smooth mixture.
5. Pour into a saucepan and stir in ketchup, salt, pepper, and basil (or sage for turkey) until combined.
6. Cook over medium heat until the gravy thickens. (Note: The gravy may not thicken completely but will have lumps of flour or cornstarch.)
7. Makes about 1 cup.
8. Serving size: 1/4 serving contains approximately 3 grams of carbohydrates, 1 gram of protein, and 16 calories.
Source: Choice Cooking, Canadian Diabetes Association (1986). Shared and tested with turkey drippings in October 1993.
